title: daphne hispanica
intro: Daphne hispanica, also known as Spanish lavender or Spanish gorse, is a flowering plant in the Daphne genus. Traditionally, the plant's roots, bark, and leaves have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Daphne hispanica include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Daphne hispanica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
